name: deep-fault-lab-checkout-payment-unreachable-agent-loop
description: A minimal bounded agent loop for checkout payment unreachable using planner-driven routing and parameterized hard nodes.

issue_type.description: Incident category inferred from trigger packet

repo_hints.description: Candidate repository roots ordered by confidence

scope_hints.description: Candidate code scopes ordered by confidence

verification_contract.description: What evidence quality is required before finish_report

scope.description: Relative path scope such as src/checkout

workdir.description: Relative working dir under repo root; use synapse for labctl

episodes.label: Signal Intake and Problem Dispatch
episodes.summary: Replay incident signal and produce inferred dispatch context (issue type, service, repo/scope hints, log strategy, verification contract).
expected_behaviors: dispatcher emits structured prior context for downstream retrieval

episodes.label: Bounded Evidence Collection
episodes.summary: Run planner-router bounded loop and evidence actions until convergence criteria are met.
expected_behaviors: router routes to matching deterministic action
snippet retrieval provides enough context for attribution
loop terminates once evidence is sufficient

nodes.name: Replay checkout payment unreachable
nodes.action: cd /home/trin/project/Synapse/deep-fault-lab/synapse && go run ./cmd/labctl replay checkout-payment-unreachable --mode grpc-direct --flag-source file --disable-after

nodes.name: Analyze trigger and dispatch context
nodes.action:
You are a bounded dispatch node for incident investigation. Read the trigger packet and emit strict JSON for downstream evidence collection.

Trigger packet:
{{replay_trigger}}

Return JSON keys:
- issue_type
- service
- repo_hints (array of candidate absolute repo roots, best-first)
- scope_hints (array of candidate relative scopes, best-first)
- log_strategy
- verification_contract
- recommended_first_query
- confidence

config.system_prompt: You are a bounded dispatcher. Do not call tools. Do not output prose. Emit strict JSON only. You must infer values from trigger packet and context. Do not rely on hardcoded scenario defaults. If uncertain, provide multiple candidates in repo_hints/scope_hints ordered by confidence and set confidence accordingly.

nodes.name: Plan next bounded step

config.system_prompt: You are a bounded Synapse planner node. You must not invent tools or execute actions. Choose one allowed next action and emit strict JSON only. Use dispatch as prior context, but all executable parameters must be emitted under action_params for the chosen action. Do not output unrelated parameters for other actions. Always include explicit values for next_action, done, reason, action_params, and report (use empty string when not finishing). If latest retrieval output is empty, change strategy instead of repeating the same query. Do not choose search_logs twice in a row unless service or query materially changes. If incident packet contains a concrete identifier such as lookup badAddress, prefer search_code with that literal. If evidence is sufficient, emit finish_report with done=true and concise report.

nodes.name: Search code by keyword
nodes.action: ROOT='{{plan.action_params.repo_root}}'; SCOPE='{{plan.action_params.scope}}'; QUERY='{{plan.action_params.query}}'; cd "$ROOT" && grep -RIn --exclude-dir .git -- "$QUERY" "$SCOPE" | head -n 20

nodes.name: Fetch Go snippet
nodes.action: ROOT='{{plan.action_params.repo_root}}'; WORKDIR='{{plan.action_params.workdir}}'; if [ -z "$WORKDIR" ]; then WORKDIR='synapse'; fi; cd "$ROOT/$WORKDIR" && if [ '{{plan.action_params.line}}' != '0' ] && [ '{{plan.action_params.line}}' != 'null' ] && [ -n '{{plan.action_params.line}}' ]; then go run ./cmd/labctl snippet go --file '{{plan.action_params.scope}}' --line '{{plan.action_params.line}}' --context-lines 18; else go run ./cmd/labctl snippet go --file '{{plan.action_params.scope}}' --symbol '{{plan.action_params.symbol}}'; fi

nodes.name: Search service logs
nodes.action: ROOT='{{plan.action_params.repo_root}}'; SERVICE='{{plan.action_params.service}}'; QUERY='{{plan.action_params.query}}'; cd "$ROOT" && docker compose logs --tail=120 "$SERVICE" | grep -n -- "$QUERY" | tail -n 20 || true
